_comment: Distilled from 'Corrective Surgery in Club Foot_Medco.pdf' and 'Corrective Surgery in Club Foot_PPD.CPD.pdf'. Note for SB062A, 'Post procedure X-ray labelled...' is 'Yes' in Medco.pdf mandatory docs but 'No' in PPD.CPD.pdf checklist. Following PPD.CPD for checklist 'expected' boolean, and Medco.pdf for mandatory_documents list.
procedure_name: Correction of club foot per cast
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 5 years' experience
clinical_indications: Congenital club foot requiring non-operative correction via manipulation and casting (Ponseti's method)

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
X-ray/MRI labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part

claim.label: Post procedure clinical photograph with cast
Post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) - affected part

ppd_preauth.q: Was the clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission submitted?
Was the X-ray/MRI labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) confirming the diagnosis submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) submitted?
Was the post procedure clinical photograph with cast submitted?
Was the Invoice/bar code of implant submitted?
Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part submitted?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes (history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management, or advice for admission) not submitted or incomplete.
X-ray/MRI not labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right), or not submitted.
Clinical photograph of affected part not submitted.
Post procedure clinical photograph with cast not submitted.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes not submitted.

clinical_key_pointers: Congenital club foot is a complex deformity that is difficult to correct and has a tendency to recur until the age of six or seven years, often associated with incomplete correction.
Untreated, children with clubfoot walk on the sides and/or top of their foot, resulting in callus formation.
The deformity has four components: Equinius, varus, adductus and cavus.
Postural or positional clubfeet are not true clubfeet.
Fixed or rigid clubfeet are either flexible (correctable without surgery) or resistant (requiring surgical release).

The main objective of corrective treatment for club foot is to reduce or eliminate the four deformities, achieving a functional, pain-free, plantigrade foot with good mobility and without calluses, not requiring modified shoes.
Initial treatment of a club foot should be non-operative, preferably manipulation and application of a plaster cast at a weekly interval.

Ponseti's method involves gentle manipulation and casting of a newborn's foot at weekly intervals, with a Treatment Phase (deformity correction) and a Maintenance Phase (brace use to prevent recurrence).
